publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-09-02 18:46:03 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: New York State Gay and Lesbian Lobby

did.physdesc: Manuscripts, graphics, news clippings, photographs, ephemera and audio visual materials.
did.abstract: Subject files, publicity items, programs for fund raising and political events, news and flyers on gay and lesbian issues; also, minutes (1991-1994), chronological files on Pride Agenda's activities, posters, t-shirts, stickers, and other ephemera relating to fund raising and political events.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: Incorporated in 1990 as the result of a merger between two statewide organizations: the New York State Lesbian and Gay Lobby based in Albany, and FAIRPAC based in New York City. The Empire State Pride Agenda is a political organization that lobbies the New York State government for legal protection and equal rights for lesbians, gay men, and bisexuals. Libby Post and Mark D'Alessio were the Pride Agenda's first co-chairs of the Board, and Richard D. Dadey, Jr., the first executive director (following Lisa Parrish as acting executive director for three months). The new organization was more successful at fundraising.

Maintaining offices both in Albany and New York City, the first issue the Pride Agenda tackled was the creation of a New York City council district drawn in a way that included enough gay and lesbian supportive voters to make the election of a lesbian or gay city councilperson a possibility. This was achieved in 1991.

The Pride Agenda adopts legislative agendas each year and has lobbied on the state level for a bias-related violence bill, a civil rights bill, and a domestic partnership bill. The organization endorses pro-gay candidates for local and state office, and remains active in New York City politics. In July 1998, New York City mayor Rudolph Giuliani signed into law legislation recognizing domestic partners in New York City.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Subject files, publicity items, programs for fund raising and political events, news and flyers on gay and lesbian issues; also, minutes (1991-1994), chronological files on Pride Agenda's activities, posters, t-shirts, stickers, and other ephemera relating to fund raising and political events. Also includes large numbers of audio visual materials such as DVDs, CD, and videos as well as the files of Joe Tarver consisting of photographs, news clippings and event files.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST

p.ref: Pride Agenda Organizational and Board Files
New York State Civil Rights Bill, 1994-1996

arrangement.p: Series III. Media Relations and Communications Boxes 3, 14-23; Mapcase folders 1-3
Series IV. Audio Visual and Digital Media Boxes 7-13

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Empire State Pride Agenda Records, #7630. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Publications removed from the collection and cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections.

list.head: Separated Publications
list.item: Pitman, Gayle. This Day in June, 2014
Wahls, Zach. My Two Moms: Lessons of Love, Strength, and What Makes a Family, 2012

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence from Senator Joseph Bruno, other correspondence between ESPA, a memo regarding 'Antidiscrimination Protections for Gay Men and Lesbians in New York State', transcript of the January 8, 1997 regular session of the Senate, news articles and informational packets

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous - Hate Crimes and Marriage Equality

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes ESPA Press Advisories/Press Releases, 'News from Attorney General Eliot Spitzer', and a letter from Anthony Crowell regarding the NYC support of marriage equality following a mayoral announcement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a Prefiled copy of the bill, a memo regarding Antidiscrimination Protections for Lesbians and Gay Men in New York State, and general information on SONDA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains the Bylaws of the Empire State Pride Agenda (x2); "The Empire State Pride Agenda's Project to Create a Statewide Organization: The Senate District Coordinator Program"; a working draft of the Strategic Plan for Election Campaign 1994; candidate surveys for Election Year 1996; assorted other documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of "Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, Two Spirit, and Transgender People of Color Health & Wellness Resource Guide" are part of this folder

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a pamphlet and printed PowerPoint from the National Black Leadership Commission on AIDS, Inc.; a copy of "AIDS in New York State: 1997 Edition" by the New York State Department of Health; "AIDS Data for the State of New York" by Reginald Fluellen for National Black Leadership Commission on Aids (1999); "HIV/AIDS and Public Health: African Americans in Crisis/A National State of Emergency" from the National Black Leadership Commission on AIDS; assorted other documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the Empire State Pride Agenda's New York State Voter Guide for November 5., 1996 (x2); a letter from the Ulster County Democrat Committee endorsing Maurice Hinchey; and a mailer from the Lambda Democrats of Bronx County.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a copy of "1998 Facts for Voters" by The League of Women Voters of New York State; a Lambda Democrats of Bronx County Voters' Guide/Palm Card; assorted Empire State Pride Agenda requests for Volunteers for the Fall 1998 cycle; various campaign materials;

scopecontent.p: She had been denied New York jobless benefits usually given to same sex couples when following a partner taking a job elsewhere. Governor Pataki stepped in and allowed the benefits.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Announcements for the 1992 Night of 100 Parties, the 1992 Pride in the Park, 1993 Awards(Fall) Dinner, 1993 Night 100, 1996 Lobby Day, 1997 Brunch (unsure if Buffalo, Ithaca, or other), and the1997 Fall Dinner

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an announcement mailer, an auction pamphlet listing items, and an event booklet.

did.unittitle: ESPA Events: "The Most Fabulous Story Ever Told", Night 100, Buffalo Tea Dance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes RSVP mailers for "The Most Fabulous Story Ever Told", postcards and RSVP mailers for Night of 100 Parties, and an RSVP mailer for the Buffalo Tea Dance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Table Captain invitations, event programs, ticket/sponsorship forms, and event booklets

did.unittitle: ESPA Events 1999 - Buffalo Brunch, Ithaca Brunch, Westchester County Dinner, 8th Fall Dinner, Holiday Party

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains mailer announcements and RSVP forms for the Buffalo Brunch, Ithaca Brunch pamphlets, a mailer for the Westchester County Dinner, announcement mailer and event booklet for the Fall Dinner

did.unittitle: Fall Dinner 1999: Journal and Ad Planning
ESPA Events 2000: Night 100, Spring Dinner, Fire Island Rites

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes invitation/announcements and RSVP forms for Night 100; postcard mailers for the Spring Dinner and Fire Island Rites

did.unittitle: ESPA Events 2000: Night 100, Buffalo Brunch, Ithaca Brunch, Spring Dinner, Break a Sweat for Pride, Hamptons Tea Dance, Rites of Summer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an postcard mailer for Night 100, an event pamphlet for the Ithaca Brunch, invitations and event pamphlets for the Buffalo Brunch, announcements and booklets for the Spring Dinner, and announcement mailers for Break a Sweat, the Hampton Tea Dance, and Rites of Summer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains postcard mailers, general invitations, and themed invitations with sponsorship forms,

did.unittitle: ESPA Events 2001: Night 100, Spring Dinner, Hamptons Tea Dance, Buffalo Brunch, Fall Dinner, Hamptons Women's Party/La Bella Luna, eBay Auction

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes general 'Save the Date' postcard mailers, postcard mailers for Night 100; postcards and an invitation/RSVP mailer for the Spring Dinner; invitation/RSVP mailers for the Hamptons Tea Dance, Buffalo Brunch, and the Fall Dinner; general and themed postcard mailers and invitation/RSVP for La Bella Luna; and a postcard mailer for the eBay auction

did.unittitle: ESPA Events 2001: Rochester Tea Dance and Rites of Summer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Save the Date postcards for 2001, postcard announcements for both the Rochester Tea Dance and the Rites of Summer, and a poster for the Rites of Summer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an invitation to the Spring Dinner and auction information for the Fall Dinner
